channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-10499] Readonly Persistent Client Cache support
item.description:
Readwrite Persistent Client Cache has been implemented by using HSM features (LU-10092). When a file is cached on Readwrite PCC (RW-PCC), only this single client can read/write to the file. RW-PCC can accelerate I/O intensive applications which read/write data into a file in a single client. However, in some other use cases, part of the files will never be updated, but need to be readed at the same time from multiple clients. Readonly Persistent Client Cache tries to implement a readonly cache level on Lustre clients using SSDs/NVMe.

Readonly Persistent Client Cache (RO-PCC) shares the same framework with Readwrite Persistent Client Cache, except that no HSM mechanism is used in readonly mode of PCC. Instead, readonly mode of PCC uses grouplock to protect the data from being written from other clients.

Whenever a file is fetched into RO-PCC, like the archive number of RW-PCC, a grouplock ID needs to be specified. The grouplock of this ID will be held in the client as long as the file is being cached in RO-PCC. Multiple clients can fetch the same file into its local cache using the same grouplock ID. Because of the semantics of grouplock, no other client can read/write from/to the file if the grouplock with the same ID is not held. Also because of the semantics of grouplock, no client can fetch the file to RO-PCC with a different grouplock ID.

status.description: This issue was once resolved, but the resolution was deemed incorrect. From here issues are either marked assigned or resolved.

comments.comment:

Commit: 31128f8f4e01a2351772d3de4daf78f12a5f44ab

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/54390/
Subject: LU-10499 pcc: avoid dead lock for auto attach in PCC-RO
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 7652ab0c7539891abdf315562992501ae4a04f98

qian_wc, I abandoned several patches that had been merged into earlier patches and already landed.

Could you please go through the patches and abandon those which have already been merged into earlier patches, even if they have not landed yet, with the Gerrit URL of which patch they were merged into. There should also be a Was-Change-Id: line in the patch that now has that fix in it, so that these fixes can be tracked properly. That will help clean up the list of outstanding PCC-RO patches and make it more clear which patches still need to land.

I can edit the commit messages to add the Was-Change-Id: lines without retriggering testing if you leave a comment on those patches, if there are no conflicts.

I think the next 4-5 PCC-RO patches look like they are ready to land, but please confirm that there are no conflicts after the recent batch of landings. If there are merge conflicts, please rebase those patches ASAP. It would also be good to get the next 5-6 patches ready to land. If they do not conflict with earlier unlanded PCC-RO patches, it would be best to rebase them directly onto master, so that they can be landed independently as much as possible, in case there is a testing problem with one of the patches.

Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 7fe6625e413919230418919479e7685f052d9086

"Qian Yingjin <qian@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55386
Subject: LU-10499 pcc: memory corruption in llapi_pcc_scan_detach()
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 3017cb7eafcd674be6b5415185815d1a4f8959ab

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/54386/
Subject: LU-10499 pcc: test interoperability with PCC-RO
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: a9445bc5d84dbf36b3de675b5f62da866d021dde

Patrick, in the case of PCC patches, in many cases there were patches later in the series that were fixing bugs in patches earlier in the series, or reworking code from earlier patches. That was unavoidable when the earlier patches were landed on b_es6_0 already, but shouldn't be propagated to master if the earlier patches were not yet landed.

I actually think the review burden is reduced by having many smaller patches, so I prefer it this way.  We need to figure out a better way of landing long patch series, but it is still easier for review to have many small patches.

I found It is hard to merge the patches that not in the patch list order, especially when the gap between two patches in the patch list is too large...
For example, the patch:
LU-10918 pcc: auto RO-PCC caching when O_RDONLY open files
It is hard to merge with the above required patches into a big one:

  • LU-10499 pcc: keep PCC copy when it is being attached (this patch is depend on the patch : LU-12373 pcc: uncache the pcc copies when remove a PCC backend)
  • LU-10499 pcc: auto attach should skip if already attached (there is no even pcc_mmap_io_init function in the patch LU-10918, the patch is depend on the patch LU-14003 pcc: rework PCC mmap implementation)
    ...

I walked through the looooong list of PCC-RO patches. There need a lot of effort to review. To reduce the effort, some of the patches can be merged together.

 


* means "feature" patch that need to be kept. Some other patches might need to
    be merged to it.
x means patch that needs to be merged to "feature" patch.
B means the patch needs to moved to the begining of the patch series.
======
* LU-10499 pcc: add lctl pcc abort command to abort attaches
    New command/feature.
B LU-10499 tests: skip sanity-pcc tests on el9.3
    All the test cases skipped is already merged since
    "LU-10499 pcc: add readonly mode for PCC".
    So this patch can be moved to the begining of the patch
    series to avoid any possible test failure.
* LU-10499 pcc: abort data copy when clear PCC backend
    Feturea patch that adds "--abort" for "lctl pcc del|clear". 
x LU-10499 tests: use smaller files in sanity-pcc/103+104
    Need to merge the change of sanity-pcc:49a,49b,49c,49d
    to the patch "LU-10499 pcc: Add dio support for data copy during attach"
    together with the patch that adds these test cases originally, i.e.
    "LU-10499 pcc: parallel data copy for attach".

    Need to merge the change of test_103/test_104 to the patch
    "LU-10499 pcc: async attach in the background for PCC-RO file"
    together with the origin patch that adds the test cases, i.e.
    "LU-10499 pcc: asynchronous PCCRO attach command support".
x LU-10499 pcc: abort data copy via ll_fid_path_copy
    Merged to "LU-10499 pcc: Add dio support for data copy during attach"
x LU-10499 pcc: abort in-progress attach by PCC detach command
    Should be merge to "LU-10499 pcc: improve pcc-detach and man pages"
x LU-10499 pcc: add --wait option for PCC detach command
    Should be merge to "LU-10499 pcc: improve pcc-detach and man pages"
x LU-10499 pcc: wait for attach finished for detach command
    Should be merge to "LU-10499 pcc: improve pcc-detach and man pages"
x LU-10499 llite: disable kernel readahead for pcc mmap
    Need to merge to "LU-14003 pcc: rework PCC mmap implementation"
x LU-10499 pcc: parallel data copy for attach
    Merged to "LU-10499 pcc: Add dio support for data copy during attach"
x LU-10499 pcc: add wait option when remove a PCC backend
    Should be merge to "LU-10499 pcc: improve pcc-detach and man pages"
x LU-10499 pcc: wait for in-progress attaches when remove PCC
    Should be merge to "LU-10499 pcc: improve pcc-detach and man pages"
* B LU-10499 tests: disable metadata_csum_seed for pcc cache device
    A simple fix of test scripts that avoid test failure. It should be
    moved to the begining of the patch series to avoid test failure.
* LU-10499 pcc: output valid state for valid cached files
    Feature patch that re-import cached file from PCC storage, can be standlone.
* LU-10499 pcc: open file for detach O_RDONLY
    Simple improvement that supports readonly mount, so can be standalone.
x LU-10499 doc: man pages for asynchronous PCCRO attachment
    Need to be merged to "LU-10499 pcc: async attach in the background for PCC-RO file"
x LU-10499 pcc: add threshold to determine direct I/O during attach
    Need to be merged to "LU-10499 pcc: Add dio support for data copy during attach"

    The part "EX-6682 build: add json-c-devel into lustre-dkms.spec.in" is not a PCC
    related test. It can be seperated to another path on the top/tail of the
    patch series.
x LU-10499 pcc: asynchronous PCCRO attach command support
    Need to be merged to "LU-10499 pcc: async attach in the background for PCC-RO file"
x LU-10499 pcc: add tunable parameter for PCC attach thread
    Need to be merged to "LU-10499 pcc: async attach in the background for PCC-RO file".
    together with patch "LU-10499 pcc: Limit attach queue depth"
x LU-10499 pcc: dio attach failed on non-blksz-aligned file
    Need to be merged to "LU-10499 pcc: Add dio support for data copy during attach"
* LU-10499 pcc: Add dio support for data copy during attach
    Major patch of dio support for PCC.
x LU-10499 pcc: Limit attach queue depth
    This is an optimization for async attach, so merge it to patch
    "LU-10499 pcc: async attach in the background for PCC-RO file".
* B LU-10499 pcc: Change PCC commands to use constants
    This patch should be moved to the begining of the patch series.
    A lot of other patches use the constant strings, e.g.
    LU-12358 pcc: add project quota support on PCC backend
    LU-14003 pcc: convert mapping pagecache for mmap
    Should avoid using string directly. Having this patch in the
    begining of the patch series can "preserve" or "predefine" the
    strings for up-coming patches.
x * LU-10499 pcc: remove realpath() from lfs_pcc_state()
    The patch is actually has two patches.
    The part of "LU-16030 pcc: enlarge PCC backend size for sanity-pcc/45"
    should be merged to "LU-10499 pcc: add test for concurrent read from 2 clients"

    The realpath() part can be kept in seperated patch.
x LU-10499 sec: fix pcc detach of encrypted file
    Should be merged to "LU-10499 sec: support of PCC-RO for encrypted files"
* LU-10499 utils: show pin information in 'lfs pcc state' command
    This can be changed to a major patch that adds more fields in "lfs pcc state"
    The patch "LU-10499 pcc: show attaching state for PCC state output" can be
    merged into this patch then.
x LU-10499 pcc: Add --pin option for 'lfs pcc attach' command
    Need to be merged to "LU-10499 utils: add pcc pin/unpin commands"
x LU-10499 pcc: tunable GID to pin/unpin files
    should be merged to "LU-10499 utils: add pcc pin/unpin commands"
x LU-10499 pcc: avoid to specify attach ID for every pin
    Need to be merged to "LU-10499 utils: add pcc pin/unpin commands"
    Pleast note "ropcc" should not be used in test script.
* LU-10499 utils: add pcc pin/unpin commands
    The major patch that adss PCC pin feature.
* LU-10499 sec: support of PCC-RO for encrypted files
    Main patch for this feature.
x LU-10499 pcc: add some statistics data
    This patch adds statistics data in /proc of pcc_attach/pcc_auto_attach, should
    be merged to "LU-10499 pcc: add stats for attach|detach|auto_attach"
* LU-10499 pcc: command to remove PCC mirror component
    A feature patch.
* LU-10499 doc: further improvements to lfs-pcc man pages
    All the manual change can be merged into a single patch at the tail
    of the path series. The benefit of it is, it is easy to read and review
    all the manual changes together. The cost is, some of the patch does not
    have proper manual. That might not be a big deal since PCC is not likely
    to work well without having the entire patch series merged.
    These patches that have manual change are:
    "LU-10499 utils: show pin information in 'lfs pcc state' command"
    "LU-10499 pcc: command to remove PCC mirror component"
    "LU-10499 pcc: improve pcc-detach and man pages"
x LU-10499 pcc: add pcc_async_affinity for async PCC attach
    Need to be merged to "LU-10499 pcc: async attach in the background for PCC-RO file"
* LU-10499 pcc: improve pcc-detach and man pages
    This is the major patch that improves pcc-detach. All the detach improvement merged
    into this patch.
x B LU-10499 pcc: make "pccro=1" default
    This patch changes a lot of "ropcc" to "pccro". Need to change the eariler patches
    to use "pccro" from the start. These patches include:
    LU-10499 pcc: add lctl pcc abort command to abort attaches
    LU-10499 pcc: Check if PCC copy is unlinked for state output
    LU-10499 pcc: print help msg more clearly for detach
    LU-10499 pcc: keep mtime unchange when attach file into PCC
    LU-10499 pcc: add pcc_mode parameter for permission check
    LU-10499 pcc: add test for concurrent read from 2 clients
    LU-10499 pcc: add stats for attach|detach|auto_attach
    LU-10499 pcc: auto attach should skip if already attached
    LU-10499 pcc: reset file mmaping for the file once mmaped
    LU-10499 pcc: avoid to specify ID for every attach
    LU-10499 pcc: add test for mmap | write | detach racer
    LU-10499 pcc: avoid dead lock for auto attach in PCC-RO
    LU-10499 pcc: mtime rule for 'lctl pcc add'
    LU-10499 pcc: async attach in the background for PCC-RO file
    LU-12358 pcc: add project quota support on PCC backend
    LU-10499 pcc: get PCC state for a file without opening itself
    LU-10499 pcc: check first before set PCC-RO on a file
    LU-13881 pcc: comparator support for PCC rules
    LU-10918 pcc: auto RO-PCC caching when O_RDONLY open files
    LU-10499 pcc: add readonly mode for PCC # Already merged
    Almost all test cases uses "ropcc", so this patch might need to be moved to
    the beginning of the patch series, i.e. pushed before
    "LU-10918 pcc: auto RO-PCC caching when O_RDONLY open files".
    And when cherry-pick the other patches, an grep of "ropcc" can
    avoid introducing new test script problem.

    The function pcc_dataset_flags_check() is added in patch
    "LU-10499 pcc: test interoperability with 2.14.0", which changes
    a series of test scripts too like this patch. So this patch
    can be merged into that patch, and moved to the beginning of the
    patch series.
x LU-10499 pcc: Check if PCC copy is unlinked for state output
    This fixes a problem when PCC copy is removed. And the patch touches
    the use case test_32 that patch
    "LU-12373 pcc: uncache the pcc copies when remove a PCC backend" adds
    So, need to merge this patch to
    "LU-12373 pcc: uncache the pcc copies when remove a PCC backend"
x LU-10499 pcc: reset file mapping for pcc copy
    Need to merge to "LU-14003 pcc: rework PCC mmap implementation"
x LU-10499 pcc: print help msg more clearly for detach
    Should be merge to "LU-10499 pcc: improve pcc-detach and man pages"

    The "Wait for async pcc attach to finish in test_47" part patch needs to be
    be merged to "LU-10499 pcc: keep mtime unchange when attach file into PCC".
* LU-10499 pcc: keep mtime unchange when attach file into PCC
    Fixes a long-time bug that does not conflict with others much.
* LU-10499 pcc: add pcc_mode parameter for permission check
    This is a feature patch. Standalone.
x LU-10499 pcc: show attaching state for PCC state output
    Need to merge to "LU-10499 utils: show pin information in 'lfs pcc state' command"
x LU-10499 pcc: set invalid cache state for fallback I/O
    Only changes a few line in pcc_file_mmap(). Need to merge it to
    Need to merge to "LU-14003 pcc: rework PCC mmap implementation"
* LU-10499 pcc: add test for concurrent read from 2 clients
    This is the patch that adds sanity-pcc:45. Part of
    "LU-10499 pcc: remove realpath() from lfs_pcc_state()" should merge into this patch/
* LU-10499 pcc: add stats for attach|detach|auto_attach
    Major patch that adss /proc entires of attach|detach|auto_attach, so standalone
    patches.
x LU-10499 pcc: auto attach should skip if already attached
    Need to merge to "LU-10918 pcc: auto RO-PCC caching when O_RDONLY open files"
x LU-10499 pcc: reset file mmaping for the file once mmaped
    Need to merge to "LU-14003 pcc: rework PCC mmap implementation"
x LU-10499 pcc: avoid uninitialized pcc mutext lock in cleanup
    The bug is introduced in "LU-1049 pcc: avoid dead lock for auto attach in PCC-RO", so
    together with that patch, merge this patch to "LU-10918 pcc: auto RO-PCC
    caching when O_RDONLY open files"
* LU-10499 pcc: avoid to specify ID for every attach
    A simple improvement that does not conflict with other patches much. So kept
    as it is.
* LU-10499 pcc: add test for mmap | write | detach racer
    This patch does not excercise any fixes of known bugs, so might be fine
    to just stay as an standalone patch.
x LU-10499 pcc: support a flatter HSM archive format
    This patch should be merged to the origin patch that addds lustre/utils/libhsm_scanner.c
    So, need to merge to "LU-12373 pcc: uncache the pcc copies when remove a PCC backend"
x LU-1049 pcc: avoid dead lock for auto attach in PCC-RO 
    Need to merge to "LU-10918 pcc: auto RO-PCC caching when O_RDONLY open files"
x LU-10499 pcc: keep PCC copy when it is being attached
    In the comment of the patch, "In this patch, we also fix a bug during
    PCC open attach. It refreshes @pcci pointer since the lock may be
    released in @pcc_try_readonly_open_attach()." That part of change should be
    merged to "LU-10918 pcc: auto RO-PCC caching when O_RDONLY open files".

    The other part of the patch, "When detach a file from PCC backend via FID,
    if the file is being attached, it should not purge the coresponding PCC
    copy from the PCC backend". This patch should be merged to patch
    "LU-10499 pcc: improve pcc-detach and man pages"
* LU-10499 pcc: don't reopen mountpoint for each cache file
    Just utility improvement, so should be one of the standalone patches.
x LU-10499 pcc: mtime rule for 'lctl pcc add'
    Need to merge to "LU-13881 pcc: comparator support for PCC rules"
x B * LU-10499 pcc: test interoperability with 2.14.0
    This patch should only adds version check for the test cases that are
    already in master branch. The version check codes in test cases added in
    un-merged patches should be splitted into the patches that add the test
    scripts.

    This patch should be merged together with "LU-10499 pcc: make "pccro=1" default"
    and moved to the begining of the patch series. See the comment in the
    patch of "LU-10499 pcc: make "pccro=1" default".
* LU-10499 pcc: async attach in the background for PCC-RO file
    This is a "feature" patch that handles all the work of async attach, so
    should be one of the standalone patches.
* LU-12358 pcc: add project quota support on PCC backend
    This is a "feature" patch that handles all the work of project quota support, so
    should be one of the standalone patches.
* LU-10499 pcc: get PCC state for a file without opening itself
    This is a "feature" patch that improves getting PCC state, so
    should be one of the standalone patches.
* LU-10499 pcc: check first before set PCC-RO on a file
    This patch includes layout lock handling on MDT side, unlike other patches that
    are mostly client-side codes. So should be one of the standalone patches.
x LU-12373 pcc: delete stale PCC copy when remove PCC backend
    Need to merge to "LU-12373 pcc: uncache the pcc copies when remove a PCC backend"
x LU-14003 pcc: convert mapping pagecache for mmap
    Need to merge to "LU-14003 pcc: rework PCC mmap implementation"
* LU-14003 pcc: rework PCC mmap implementation
    This is a "feature" patch that handles all the work of mmap support, so
    should be one of the standalone patches.
* LU-13881 pcc: comparator support for PCC rules
    This is a "feature" patch that handles all the work of attach rules, so
    should be one of the standalone patches.
* LU-12373 pcc: uncache the pcc copies when remove a PCC backend
    This is a "feature" patch that handles all the work of removing a PCC
    backend, so should be one of the standalone patches.
* LU-10918 pcc: auto RO-PCC caching when O_RDONLY open files 
    This is a "feature" patch that handles all the work of auto-caching, so
    should be one of the standalone patches.
* LU-11829 pcc: Use different fnames for RW-PCC and RO-PCC modes
    This patch is missing from the series. I think it should be one of the
    first patches in the series. The following patche calls 
    lpcc_fid2path() with "readonly" option:
    LU-12373 pcc: uncache the pcc copies when remove a PCC backend

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/38305/
Subject: LU-10499 pcc: add readonly mode for PCC
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: ce98bfe5f72657f807201fc7eadae2fee07d7831

I have already merged lots of bug fixes into the original patch. And I also marked those that does not merge into the original one by comment message in the Gerrit. (The original patchset (PCC-RO) is more than 100 before merged.)
I will refine the patchset and make it more reasonable later on.

Yingjin, it looks like there are 67 patches for the PCC-RO code so far. How many of those are bug fixes that could be merged into the original patch, or have you been merging bug fix patches already? I'm happy to review smaller patches that are focused on a specific feature, but managing so many patches will also take time and effort, so it makes sense to avoid extra patches that could be merged into the original code without making it (much) larger.

Yingjin Qian (qian@ddn.com) uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/32021
Subject: LU-10499 llite: fix problem of fetch
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 83af1ef273fe694df1fbbf7d4cc3cbb33f803194

Currently Readonly mode of PCC uses grouplock to protect the data from being written or even normal read from other clients, even if there is still a copy on the original OSTs.
The patch above introduces a new kind of LDLM lock mode PCCRO, which is compatible with PR lock mode (but not compatible with PW and GROUP lock mode), so when the file is PCC-fetched and attached on a client, the client will obtain PCCRO lock on the file, the PCCRO lock is kept until the administrator detachs the PCC-fetched file. It allows the read access for the file data on original OSTs from other clients, but no write lock (PW) is allowed.

Yingjin Qian (qian@ddn.com) uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/31868
Subject: LU-10499 llite: Add new DLM mode PCCRO for readonly PCC
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: a6e8e5b5108168cfb907f23122ce2ef0c6a37822

> The one problem with the grouplock is that this will be dropped when the filesystem is restarted. Secondly, the grouplock prevents other clients from reading the file, even if there is still a copy on the original OST.

Agreed. However, I don't think dropping the lock when the file system is restarted is a big problem. Remounting the client doesn't happen a lot.

In order to solve the problem that other clients can't reading the PCC cached files, we can either:

1) Add readonly support for the grouplock. When some on is holding the readonly grouplock of the file, no write lock is allowed, and only readlock can be granted.

2) Add none-copy support for readonly PCC. When a client is accessing the file which is cached by other clients on readonly PCC, the client will require the PCC grouplock automatically, but will not copy the data to local storage. Instead, the client will read from the OSTs directly.

Either way should work well. But I'd prefer solution 2), unless solution 1) can help other use cases since solution 1) will be much harder.

> What I think would be a better mechanism for this would be to leverage the FLR functionality and state machine directly for the PCC mirrors.

I don't have any idea about how to use FLR for PCC. Can you explain more details?

The one problem with the grouplock is that this will be dropped when the filesystem is restarted. Secondly, the grouplock prevents other clients from reading the file, even if there is still a copy on the original OST.

What I think would be a better mechanism for this would be to leverage the FLR functionality and state machine directly for the PCC mirrors.

Li Xi (lixi@ddn.com) uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/30847
Subject: LU-10499 llite: add readonly support for PCC
